name: Golden Sweet
image_alt: Flat yellow snow pea pods on climbing vines
description: A striking Indian heirloom snow pea with flat, entirely edible pods that mature golden-yellow rather than green. Sweeter than typical snow peas with a tender, crisp texture.
history: An heirloom from India where yellow-podded peas have been grown for centuries. Brought to American seed savers through heirloom exchanges in the 1990s. Rare but increasingly popular for its flavor and visual appeal.
seasonal_quirks: Similar growing requirements to other snow peas but slightly more heat-tolerant. Yellow color makes pods easy to spot at harvest — nothing hides in the foliage. Cool-season crop.
harvest_cues: Pods should be flat — any noticeable pea bumps indicate slightly past peak. Golden-yellow, 3–4 inches long, snapping cleanly.
variety_notes: The yellow pods fade to tan when cooked — use raw or very briefly stir-fried for maximum color impact. Excellent in stir-fries where the yellow provides visual contrast.
preservation: Blanch briefly and freeze. Quick pickle in rice vinegar.
